intro: The clearest difference between Airbrake and Sentry is lock-in — Airbrake has medium lock-in (Error data exportable; integrations portable to Sentry), whereas Sentry has low lock-in (Low — open-source, self-hosted option, OTel-compatible). Beyond that, developer experience — Airbrake scores 4/5 against Sentry's 5/5. Airbrake fits mobile; Sentry fits error tracking and crash reporting for any platform. On the strengths side, Airbrake is cited for mobile and web apps in production, while Sentry is cited for error tracking and crash reporting for any platform. The honest trade-off: Airbrake is the wrong call for pre-launch, Simple projects; Sentry is the wrong call for full APM with infrastructure monitoring; Airbrake users flag Pre-launch/beta apps, and Sentry users flag full APM with infrastructure monitoring. Choosing between them should come down to the constraint that actually binds your workflow, not surface-level overlap. On developer experience the data shows Airbrake at 4/5 and Sentry at 5/5, with transparency at 4/5 and 4/5 respectively.
quick_take: Airbrake is for mobile; Sentry is for error tracking and crash reporting; decide based on lock-in tolerance.
verdict_a: Choose Airbrake if your use case is mobile, and medium lock-in is acceptable in exchange for its fit.
verdict_b: Choose Sentry if your use case is error tracking and crash reporting for any platform, and low lock-in is acceptable in exchange for its fit.

faq.a: Both Airbrake and Sentry use a freemium model with a free tier, so raw pricing is close on paper. The real cost difference depends on volume, seats, and which paid features you actually need. Check current pricing on each vendor's page before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Airbrake to Sentry?
faq.a: Migrating from Airbrake to Sentry is feasible when their scopes overlap, but the work is in re-mapping configuration, data models, and integrations. Moving off Airbrake is harder because of medium lock-in (Error data exportable; integrations portable to Sentry). Plan for a dual-run period so you can validate parity before decommissioning.

On our developer-experience score, Sentry is rated 5/5 and Airbrake 4/5, so Sentry edges ahead. Scores like this smooth over real differences in docs, CLI feel, and SDK quality, so treat them as a starting point. Spend an afternoon in each before relying on the number.

faq.q: Is Sentry a good alternative to Airbrake?
faq.a: Sentry is a reasonable alternative to Airbrake when Sentry fits error tracking and crash reporting for any platform. If your use case matches Airbrake's sweet spot — mobile — staying with Airbrake is probably safer. Airbrake itself is not meant for pre-launch, Simple projects, which is often where users start shopping for alternatives.
